problem.value: Avoid missed handoffs at mission end: enable renewal decisions, offboarding, and timely final invoicing
problem.evidence: Request only states the desired notification, not the underlying risk (missed renewal? billing gap? compliance?)

current_process.evidence: Not mentioned — likely manual tracking via calendar/spreadsheet today

actors.value: Freelancer (subject), Client contact(s) with operational/HR role, internal account manager — 'right people' undefined
actors.evidence: 'notify the right people' names no roles; context notes routing depends on Contact.role

business_objects.value: Mission (has start/end date), Freelancer, Client, Contact
business_objects.evidence: 'freelancer's mission' explicitly named; Client/Contact inferred from domain model

business_rules.value: Threshold for 'about to end' (e.g. 30/15/7 days before) not defined; may vary by client or contract type
business_rules.evidence: 'about to end' is undefined — no lead time given, and rules commonly vary by client/contract per context notes

workflow.value: Mission lifecycle: active → approaching-end (new trigger point) → ended; single vs recurring reminders unclear
workflow.evidence: End-of-mission is a known event per context; 'about to end' implies a new pre-end trigger not yet in the model

integrations.value: Notification channel (email/in-app/SMS) and trigger mechanism (scheduled job vs event) undefined
integrations.evidence: Date-based 'about to end' strongly suggests an async scheduled check, but channel/frequency not specified

permissions.evidence: Not addressed; who can view/edit mission end dates, mute or reroute notifications is undefined

config_vs_custom.evidence: Platform is configurable per client (approval circuits, notification routing); unclear if this rule is global or per-client

constraints.evidence: No deadline, budget or regulatory driver mentioned

edge_cases.evidence: Mission extended/cancelled/renewed after notification sent not addressed; multiple freelancers ending same day

reporting.evidence: No compliance/audit driver named for this specific feature

risks.evidence: Not defined; risk of duplicate/spam notifications or missed ones not discussed

questions.q: Who exactly are 'the right people'? Freelancer's internal manager, the client's operational/billing contact, HR, someone else — and does this vary by client or contract?
questions.why: uncertainty (25%, inferred) × high impact: this defines the whole routing logic and permission model; guessing wrong means rebuilding notification targeting later

questions.q: How far in advance counts as 'about to end' — a fixed lead time (e.g. 15 days) or configurable per client/contract? One reminder or a series (30/15/7 days)?
questions.why: uncertainty (5%, empty) × high impact: the threshold and its variability directly size the rule engine and scheduling logic

questions.q: Is this notification rule the same for every client, or must it be configurable per client/contract like other routing rules on the platform?
questions.why: uncertainty (0%, empty) × high impact: determines whether this is a hardcoded trigger or a configurable rule engine — very different build costs

questions.q: What should trigger downstream from this notification — e.g. should it also prompt final invoicing, mission-end validation, or a renewal decision workflow?
questions.why: uncertainty (15%, inferred) × high impact: end-of-mission is a known automation trigger point (invoicing, validation); scope here changes if notification is standalone or the start of a chain

questions.q: Who should be able to see, edit, mute, or reroute these notifications (e.g. can a client contact turn them off, can a manager reassign)?
questions.why: uncertainty (0%, empty) × high impact: permissions are a known blind spot in this platform, surfacing late at acceptance testing if unaddressed now

questions.q: Should this run as a scheduled daily/nightly check against mission end dates, or be tied to a specific real-time event — and via which channel (email, in-app, both)?
questions.why: uncertainty (10%, empty) × medium-high impact: sync vs async trigger design changes error handling, retry logic and UX substantially

summary.objective: Automatically alert the relevant people before a freelancer's mission ends, so action (renewal, offboarding, final invoicing) can be taken in time.
summary.scope: Likely: a scheduled check against Mission end dates, a configurable lead-time threshold, role-based routing to freelancer manager and/or client contacts, and a notification (email/in-app). Possibly triggers downstream actions like final invoicing or mission-end validation.
assumptions: The problem is to avoid missed renewal/offboarding/invoicing decisions, not just a generic reminder
The mission end date already exists as a field on the Mission object
The trigger is date-based (scheduled/async), not tied to another real-time event
Notification routing may need to vary by client/contract, consistent with other configurable rules on this platform
'Right people' includes at least an internal manager and possibly a client-side contact by role
summary.blind_spot: The request assumes 'the right people' is obvious, but on this platform notification routing depends on Contact roles and can vary by client/contract — without defining who and under what rule, the team risks building a single hardcoded recipient list that has to be reworked per client, and missing the fact that mission-end is also a common trigger for final invoicing and validation workflows.
